description: A top-tier deep-dive course for learners who have completed Kubernetes Intermediate 2. Covers extending the Kubernetes API with CRDs and Operators, enforcing policies with Admission Control, configuring advanced autoscaling with VPA, Cluster Autoscaler, and KEDA, automating deployments with GitOps (ArgoCD) and progressive delivery (Canary and Blue-Green), and mastering traffic management, mTLS, and observability with the Istio service mesh.
summary: Extend the API with CRDs and Operators, enforce Admission policies, configure advanced autoscaling (VPA, KEDA), adopt GitOps with ArgoCD, implement progressive delivery, and operate the Istio service mesh — a deep-dive course that takes your Kubernetes operations to the next level.
target_audience: Learners who have completed Kubernetes Intermediate 2 (already familiar with RBAC, scheduling, NetworkPolicy, Helm, and monitoring)

lectures.title: CRDs and Custom Resources — Extending the Kubernetes API
objectives: Understand the structure of a CustomResourceDefinition (CRD) and OpenAPI v3 schema validation, and define your own CRD
Create custom resources and manage them with kubectl get/describe/explain just like built-in resources
Explain what it means to extend the Kubernetes API and how CRDs operate on the same foundation as built-in resources

lectures.title: The Operator Pattern and Controllers — Encoding Operational Knowledge
objectives: Understand the reconciliation loop and how a controller continuously converges actual state toward desired state
Install an existing Operator and observe the controller reacting to custom resource changes
Explain key Operator development concepts including the Operator SDK and the Operator maturity model

lectures.title: Admission Control and Policy Enforcement — Webhooks, OPA/Kyverno, and Pod Security
objectives: Understand where Validating and Mutating Admission Webhooks sit in the API request processing pipeline
Define policies with OPA Gatekeeper or Kyverno to block or mutate non-compliant resources
Apply Pod Security Admission to enforce security standards at the namespace level

lectures.title: Advanced Autoscaling — VPA, Cluster Autoscaler, and KEDA
objectives: Understand how Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA) automatically adjusts Pod requests/limits and explain its differences from HPA
Understand how Cluster Autoscaler scales nodes up and down at the infrastructure level
Configure KEDA to scale workloads based on event-driven metrics such as queue length or message count

lectures.title: GitOps and ArgoCD — Declarative Continuous Delivery
objectives: Understand GitOps principles and the concept of Git as the single source of truth
Configure an ArgoCD Application to synchronize a Git repository with cluster state
Observe drift detection, self-healing, and automated sync behavior in action

lectures.title: Progressive Delivery — Canary, Blue-Green, and PodDisruptionBudget
objectives: Understand the differences between Canary, Blue-Green, and other progressive deployment strategies and when to apply each
Use Argo Rollouts to split traffic progressively and deploy a new version in stages
Explain how a PodDisruptionBudget (PDB) preserves availability during deployments and node drains

lectures.title: Introduction to Service Mesh — Traffic Management, mTLS, and Observability with Istio
objectives: Understand the sidecar proxy-based service mesh architecture and the distinction between the data plane and control plane
Control traffic routing with Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule, and apply automatic mTLS encryption
Leverage the observability capabilities (distributed tracing, metrics) provided by a service mesh
